task_completion.description: The agent entered the required search query into the Splunk search bar.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk Search & Reporting interface is visible, and the search bar contains the query: index=security_logs "Failed password" | stats count by src_ip | where count > 5.

task_completion.description: The agent opened the dialog to save the search as an alert.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save As' dropdown is clicked and 'Alert' is selected, opening the 'Save As Alert' configuration modal/dialog.
In the 'Save As Alert' dialog, the 'Title' or 'Name' field contains exactly 'Brute_Force_Detection'.

task_completion.description: The agent configured the alert to run on a 5-minute cron schedule.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the alert configuration dialog, the schedule type is set to 'Cron Schedule' and the cron expression field contains '*/5 * * * *'.

task_completion.description: The agent configured the alert to trigger when results are greater than 0.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the alert configuration dialog, the trigger condition is set to 'Number of Results' 'is greater than' '0'.

task_completion.description: The agent successfully saved the alert.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success modal stating 'Your alert has been created' or 'Alert has been saved' is visible, or the 'Brute_Force_Detection' alert is visible in the Splunk 'Alerts' list tab.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Splunk Web UI to create the alert.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the Splunk web interface in a browser (http://localhost:8000), rather than using terminal commands (like curl) to interact with the Splunk API.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual Splunk application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The visual elements match the standard Splunk Enterprise dashboard and dialogs, not a text editor or a mock HTML page.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the UI by directly editing Splunk configuration files.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent opening terminal windows to edit files like 'savedsearches.conf' to manually inject the alert configuration.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target alert name must be exactly 'Brute_Force_Detection'. The search query must include 'index=security_logs' and 'Failed password'. The schedule must be a cron expression exactly matching '*/5 * * * *'. The alert must be scheduled (is_scheduled=true) and trigger when the number of results is > 0.
